description: Apollo 16 Flight Journal - Day 10, Part 1: Preparation for EVA

tokens: This is Apollo Control, Houston, at 237 hours, 29 minutes [212:43] Ground Elapsed Time.
We now show Apollo 16 at a distance of 187,791 nautical miles away from the Earth.
And now travelling at a speed of 3,750 feet per second relative to the Earth.
In the Mission Control Center we've had a change of shift.
Our Flight Director at this time is Phil Shaffer and filling the Capcom position at this time Henry Hartsfield.
We'll stand by now with the line open awaiting for a crew callup.
We're at 237 hours, 30 minutes [212:44:] Ground Elapsed Time and this is Apollo Control, Houston, continuing to monitor.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: The PAO, Mission Control and Crew are using the updated GET, intended to align events to the Flight Plan.
At this stage in the flight, the difference from the actual GET is 24 hours, 45 minutes and 52 seconds.
transcript.speaker: Commentary

tokens: Is everybody wide awake and feeling great this morning?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: You even gave us an extra hour.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We're coming up on antenna switch, and I think the comm is a little bad; but, right off the bat we'd like for you to remain in PTC.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And we'd also like for you to take the Up Telemetry Command switch Off for three seconds.
We've got that same commanding problem we had during coast out to the Moon.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: It's Off and back to Normal.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: That cured up the commanding problem.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Hank, before we copy the Flight Plan updates, do you want us to start on the 236-hour item?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We'd like for you to get started on the postsleep checklist.
And, rather than read you a bunch of things to start with, I think I'll just remind you of a few things to catch you up.
I've got a list of them here.
While you're doing that, if we could get somebody to pull the Gamma Ray boom in, I'd appreciate it.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: You want that all the way in?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And you'll need your Logic Powers to get that in.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And we'd like to get the S-Band Normal Mode switch to Voice.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We couldn't talk to you if we weren't in Voice, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We were in Down Voice Backup there and then Command in Normal.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And, Ken, for your information, we've scrubbed the corona window calibration and the Skylab contamination photos.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And we're gonna stay at PTC right up to the Mid-Course 5.
And if you're - can get to it now, Ken, I'd like to get the Image Motion, On; and the Mapping Camera, On.
And, then give me barber pole plus two.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Hank, you say you want the Mapping Camera Image Motion, On; and then the cameras on [garble].
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We want to get the Image Motion, On; the Mapping Camera, On; barber pole plus two.
And we're gonna leave it shut up in there.
We're just running the film out.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: This is Apollo Control, Houston, at 237 hours, 37 minutes [212:50] Ground Elapsed Time.
The crew of Apollo 16 [have been] given some extra sleep time this morning.
Two of the earlier items scheduled have been deleted from the Flight Plan, the corona calibration photography and the Skylab contamination photography.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: We're at 237 hours 38 minutes [212:51] Ground Elapsed Time.
We show Apollo 16 at a distance of 187,462 nautical miles away from the Earth and travelling at a speed of 3,755 feet per second relative to the Earth.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: For your information, old mother Earth's got you now, and you're coming home.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We're just now showing you crossing under the Earth's in - sphere of influence.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: That was Capcom Henry Hartsfield advising the crew of Apollo 16 that they're now in the Earth's sphere of influence.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Mid-Course 5 will be about 4 feet per second.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We show 1 hour, 41 minutes till time of the Mid-Course burn.
This with the Delta V of about 4 foot per second performed with a [sic] Reaction Control System.
We're looking presently at a burn duration of 8 to 9 seconds.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: We now show Apollo 16 187,381 nautical miles away from the Earth.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Hank, would you say again what you wanted done with this Mapping Camera, please?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We just want to run the film out and leave the door shut.
We want to get the Image Motion, On; Mapping Camera, On; and barber pole plus 2.
That gives us a higher speed.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Whenever you get a chance, we'd like to get yesterday's crew status and today's crew status report.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: When you get a break there, I do have a few Flight Plan changes I'd like to get up.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Let me finish this status report thing.
It all uses the same book.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Ken, we need the Gamma Ray Boom switch Off.
It didn't quite make it all in.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We'd like to verify that you heard my transmission on holding PTC to Mid-Course 5.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Roger, and for planning purposes, we'd like to get a P52 in sometime in the next 25 minutes.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Well, for work purposes, Ken is putting up the optics now.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Here's the crew status report, starting with the - A - item A-1 for the Commander.
It's in the pressure suit, which is in the bag, and it's down - and it's very dusty, and I'd like to wait until we get ready to suit up for the EVA and I'll read you the PRD then.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: A-3: Day 8, 7 hour - 7 hours, good sleep, nope.
Day 9: still skip the PRD reading for the same reason; 6¾ hours, good sleep.
On the LMP, day 8: skip the PRD, and suit was out of the way then.
And, Charlie remembered to get his PRD reading - reading machine out today, and C-1 is 21143.
C-3: 7 hours, good sleep, C-4: none.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Did we lose you doing any of that, Hank?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: When we got back to the - to good old Casper here - We're working on Day 8, meal C.
And for the CDR, substitute a hamburger for beef gravy, and scratch the gingerbread.
And for day 9, Meal A: scratch the peaches and the grits.
Day 9, Meal B: scratch the hamburger and add meatballs with sauce.
For day 9, Meal C: scratch the pecans.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: For the LMP, with all of Day 8, scratch the mixed fruit, the ham steak, the white bread with jelly.
On meal B: scratch the pea soup, the meatballs, the pork, and escalloped potatoes.
Meal C: scratch the chocolate bar and a gingerbread and add pi - pineapple and a hamburger.
On Day 9; scratch the peaches, the scrambled eggs, and the bacon squares on meal A.
On Meal B: scratch the hamburger and white bread, the instant breakfast and the cereal bar.
Meal C: scratch the pecans only.
On the Duke: [sic], Day 8, last meal, scratch the beef and gravy and substitute a hamburger.
And, scratch two-thirds of a chocolate bar.
On Day 9, Meal A: scratch the peaches.
And on Meal B: scratch the hamburger and - in a wetpack and the - and the cereal bar.
On day 9, Meal C: scratch the pecans only and add an orange drink.
I think that about does it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: The surgeon's happy, and we didn't get any biomed on you last night.
Or, you have any idea why it didn't work?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: I sure had it plugged in.
I had it plugged in the night before last, too.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We're getting a - the carrier, John.
So, there must be something fouled up in the system, there.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Well, why didn't you tell me, I could have changed this sensor out' - or this - it's probably in this box here, don't you reckon?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: When you get a chance, John, just check it out.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And, we were - we lost our data on the antenna switching with your P52.
Could we get those angles, please?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: You - you have any idea what it might be that I could do to fix this ...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We just went through an antenna switch there, and I lost anything you said, then.

And, John, if you're asking about the sensor there, the only thing we could suggest is to check the connections there into the blue signal conditioner and maybe change out your sensors or service them.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: I can't - I can't believe that if you got the carrier that changing out the sensors is going to change anything.
These things are really stuck on there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We got us a signal there.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And what - what angles are you showing on your High Gain now, on your meter?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Showing - 50 - minus 50 in Pitch - make it 45, and about -
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: They want to know now what's on the knobs.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: It's - right now, it's reading minus - We'll get the Pitch, minus 40 and minus 40 on the knob - and it's reading 90 Degrees in Yaw and still about 265 in Yaw in - on the indicator.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And, 16, we'd like to get that P52 data.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We've got stars 50 and 22; Noun 05: all zeroes; Noun 93: minus 0.040, plus 0.030, plus 0.058; torqued at 213:23:55.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And, 16, whenever you're ready, we'll give you a few of these changes.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: The Shield's Off right now, Hank.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We just turned it Off, Hank.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: In that next group of data there, delete anything that refers to the Mass Spec, since we don't have it any more.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And, about 238:50 [214:03], we want Gamma Ray Shield, On.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And, the group of data concerning the P30.
We want to start the P30, actually, around 238:46.
But, we'll have to get you up-linked, and we're working on that now.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And, you'll exit PTC prior to that - just put that in just ahead of the P30.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Exit PTC right in front of the P30.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Using the SIM bay jett configuration.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And, as soon as we get the sextant star check there on over at 239, we'll go ahead and get the dumps out of the way.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We'll start the dumps right after the star check.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: At 239:30 [214:43], those High Gain angles should be minus 16 and 58.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And if you'll flip on over to page 351.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: As we call - we need another hour for suit donning.
So, that time there at 241, at the top of the page, will now be 242 [217:13].
So, there's actually an hour's gap between the previous page and this one.
And, you can change your time accordingly, adding an hour on to - You get down to hatch opening or until you get on the EVA cue card.
And, then we'll pick up the Flight Plan after the EVA.
Now, at 242 hours - that's after it's changed there where it says Verb 49 maneuver to EVA attitude, the new attitude is 037, 043, 038, and the High Gain angles are minus ...
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We change Verb from 49, angles to 037.
The other two are the same.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: They just changed that Yaw angle while I was reading it up.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Down about - a few lines down where it says, "Load MEED attitude - that attitude now is 071, 051, 039.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And if you'll flip the page now - Page 352 - the new time there is 243:40 [218:53].
Where it says, "Maneuver to MEED attitude," we want to change that again to 071, 051, 039, and the High Gain angles are minus 34, 218.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: At the - this whole page has slipped an hour, and it's at 243:40 [218:53]; 071, 051, 039, to minus 34 and a 218.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And, we need to make those - those appropriate changes on the cue card and then the EVA Checklist.
And, I - I have those locations, if you want to do that.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Is that all you've got for now?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That ought to take us through the EVA, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: On the High Gain, go Manual and Wide, and then Reacq Narrow.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: If you read, Manual Wide and then Reacquire Narrow.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: This is Apollo Control, Houston, at 238 hours, 36 minutes [213:49] Ground Elapsed Time.
We now show Apollo 16 at a distance of 185,317 nautical miles away from the Earth and travelling at a speed of 3,783 feet per second and we've been experiencing some noisy comm with Apollo 16.

Meanwhile, Mid-Course Correction number 5 is presently scheduled for 239 hours, 20 minutes [and] 56 seconds [214:34:54] Ground Elapsed Time with a Delta V of 3.4 feet per second, [and] a burn duration of eight seconds.
This will be performed with the Reaction Control System thrusters, posigrade to change the flight path angles from minus 8.6 to minus 6.5 degrees.
We presently show Apollo 16 with a spacecraft weight of 27,409 pounds, at 238 hours, 37 minutes [213:50] Ground Elapsed Time, [and] continuing to monitor, this is Apollo Control, Houston.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: I have your Mid-Course 5 PAD, and if you'll give us Accept we'll up-link the target load and vector.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And we're also sending you a PIPA bias with that load.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Hank, go ahead with the PAD.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: MCC-5, RCS/G&N; 27409; Noun 48 is NA; 239:20:55.86; Noun 81 plus 0003.4, all zips, all zips; 079, 306, 346; HA is NA, plus 0021.7; 0003.4, 0:09, 0003.4; 04, 047.9, 39.1; boresight star 056, up 27.0, left 3.5; and for Ken's information, that's Beta Centauri, and it's on the white chart and it lies between Atria and Acrux.
Continue with Noun 61, minus 00.73, minus 156.19; 1046.3, 36277; 290:24:04; Sirius and Rigel; 219, 166, 313; four jets, plus-X.
High gain angles: pitch, minus 90; yaw, 285; and a note that the EMS is not bias for drift.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We copy Mid-Course 4 [sic], RCS/G&N, 27409; NA for Noun 48; 239:20:55.86; plus 0003.4, plus all balls, plus all balls; 079, 306, 346; HA is NA, plus 0021.7; 0003.4, 0:08; 0003.4; Oh, 047.9, 39.1; 056, up 270, left 35; Beta Centauri, minus 00.73, minus 156.19; 1046.3, 36277; 290:24:04, Sirius and Rigel; 219, 166, 313; four jets, plus-X.
High gain: plus pitch, minus 90; yaw, minus 255 - pitch, minus 90; yaw is 285; EMS is not bias, for drift.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And we'd like to get the Gamma Ray, shield on.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: The Gamma Ray shield is now shielding.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We're having a little trouble getting the High Gain to acquire, so we'll be calling you Omni switches, so we can maintain voice.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Hank, I had a question here.
You said to exit the - use the SIM bay jett configuration.
Then, I notice where you called out for a plus-X four jet on the Mid-Course.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: What happened there, Ken, was we were gonna do a Gamma Ray extension for a little - out a little ways, and we've - we've cancelled that now, so that's not even a requirement anymore.
You can use normal jet configuration.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Okay; you have Omni Alpha with a Command Reset.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And, Hank, can I go - go ahead and stop PTC passing 79 degrees roll, this time?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And how about the status of the computer?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: How big is the Moon this morning?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Haven't gotten around to looking yet, Hank.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, Hank, at arm's distance, if you cup your fingers around it, it's about as big as a 50-cent piece.
But you can sure tell that it's changed size for the smaller, but we still feel like we're still pretty close to it.
Like I - I don't know how far away from it we are, but, probably about - maybe 25 or 30,000 miles.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Take it back; they rewrote about 38,000.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And:, 16, when you get the attitude, we'd like for you to bring up the High Gain, and you're there.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: At 238 hours, 59 minutes [214:13] Ground Elapsed Time; we now show Apollo 16 at a distance of 184,476 nautical miles away from the Earth.
Now travelling at a speed of 3,795 feet per second.
That was John Young that you heard providing a description of the moon as he viewed it out the cabin window.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Henry, how do you read on the High Gain?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Reading you five by five by 5, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Okay, you're in Reacq in error.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: This is Apollo Control, Houston, we're 20 minutes away now from time of ignition from the Mid-Course Correction 5 scheduled at 239 hours, 20 minutes [and] 56 seconds [214:34:54].
This will be a burn that will provide a Delta V of 3.4 feet per second with a duration of 8 seconds.
Done with the RCS pulsic rate [?] that will change the flight path angle from minus 8.6 degrees to minus 6.5 degrees.
We're at 239 hours, 2 minutes [214:15] Ground Elapsed Time.
Apollo 16 now 184,380 nautical miles away from the Earth.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: We're getting close to that Mid-Course, and we'd like to go ahead and get the dumps out of the way.
Platform alignment looks good, so you can omit the star check, if you like.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We just looked at the boresight star.
We're not going to mess with the sextant check, and we're in process of dumping.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Alpha and Beta [Centauri] are two of the prettier stars you can find.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Ken, we got a list of questions here on the SIM bay inspection - when you get out on the EVA, and we can handle them any way you like.
It's about a page and a half of them.
Would you rather I read them after you're out or ...
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: No, I'd like to hear them first, but, not right this minute.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I'll give you a call in just a minute when we get squared away here.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I just wanted to apprise you of the fact that I had them, and then whenever you're ready we'll talk about them.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Did you have any trouble with your LiOH canister change today?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: 16, we show you about 10 percent on the waste tank.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: This is Apollo Control, Houston at 239 hours, 12 minutes [214:25] Ground Elapsed Time, less than 10 minutes away now from the Mid-Course Correction number 5 burn, and we presently show the onboard computer in Program 30.
This is the external Delta V program which is used for engine ignitions.
We're at 239 hours, 12 minutes [214:25] Ground Elapsed Time and Apollo 16 now 183,995 nautical miles away from the Earth.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: This is Apollo Control, Houston, the onboard computer now in Program 41 of the SC RCS program to provide a [correct] attitude for an RCS thrusting maneuver.
We're now seven minutes away from time of ignition for Mid-Course Correction number 5.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Hey, I've put on some new sensors.
How about seeing if your friends over on the left there are happy with that.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Apollo Control, Houston, two minutes away now from time of ignition from Mid-Course Correction number 5.
We're at 239 hours 19 minutes [214:32] Ground Elapsed Time.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Flight Director, Phil Shaffer, taking a final status check in Mission Control for Mid-Course 5.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: You're looking good, and you're Go for the burn.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: One minute away now from Mid-Course 5.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Apollo 16 now 183,706 nautical miles away from the Earth.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Standing by now for burn status.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Okay, Houston, I guess as you saw, the burn was nominal.
Our residuals: minus 110, plus 0, minus 110; and you probably saw our Noun 20 for the attitude.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That was John Young reporting a nominal burn on Mid-Course Correction number 5.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Hank, you want to leave the Mapping Camera on; is that correct?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And Ken, the Mapping Camera is out of film, but the Stellar Camera is still feeding a little.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We gave it lots of stars to look at, just then.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Hank, is the High Gain gonna track us to this maneuver, or should we set it Manual and Reacquire when we ...
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Seems to be tracking good now.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Hey, I'm going to start Battery B Charge.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Apollo 16 now 183,521 nautical miles away from the Earth.
Velocity now reading 2,809 feet per second.
Mid-Course Correction 5 are [sic] completed and as was reported by Apollo 16 a nominal on time burn.
We're at 239 hours, 25 minutes continuing to monitor.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Hey, Hank, when we were allowed to sleep through the eat period there, did we move the EVA back or anything like that?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: The EVA is - at the nominal time - including that hour, you know, that we had to put in there.
One-hour slip from what was originally in the Flight Plan.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We just traded off eating and sleeping this morning, is that it?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We were hoping you were grabbing some snacks in there early this morning.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Man, with three guys in here, Hank, you just - when it comes time to eat, you just bring everything to a halt and go do that that's - because everything has to be done in the kitchen.
You got to take your 52s and you got to do everything else right in one place.
We'll grab something and we'll be pressing on here; we'll - we'll probably come pretty close to the schedule.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, we're scheduled for hatch opening at 242:55 [218:08].
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Okay; we'll - we'll try to be there, and if we don't make it there, why, we'll get it as soon as we get there.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Hey, Hank, we just looked at - at the battery compartment pressure.
You want us to vent that thing?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And I can copy your comments about the SIM bay now if you'd like to give them now, while ...
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: In regard to the Mapping Camera, they want to check the interference.
Inspect the periphery of the Mapping Camera and Laser Altimeter for any evidence of interference.
The last attraction on that thing was nominal ...
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: so it's kind of puzzling, you know.
It started out real slow and each retraction it got faster.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Some of the things they suggest you might look for is - around the cable, there, between the Mapping Camera and the shelf, is there any damage to the cable?
Clearance in that area of the cable?
And whether the cable is caught or curled ...
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Now, that cable is located on the - on the plus-X side of the camera over on the - towards the A quad, is that correct?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We want to know if that cable is caught or curled or is it smooth, you know, like it's supposed to?
That's between the - what I would call the top of the Mapping Camera and the bay, there, by the handhold.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: I'm not sure we're gonna have enough light to see down in there in the EVA attitudes but we'll take a look at it.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And we'd like to know a little something about the cuckoo door.
You know which one that is?
The little white door that goes over the stellar lens?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: I won't be able to tell you much about that in the retracted position, either.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: If possible, we'd like for you to lift up that door and inspect the clearance between the hinge and the top of the Mapping Camera, and look for any scrape marks on the camera or on the door and any evidence of twisting or bending of the stellar lens glare shield.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Any question on that part with reference to the Mapping Camera, Ken?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Did you have any question about any of those Mapping Camera items?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: In regard to the Pan Camera, we'd like for you to look at the exposure sensor.
And that's the ones that's immediately to the left of the lens barrel.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And we need Auto on the High Gain.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: In - In regard to that automatic exposure control sensor there, Ken, what we're looking for is foreign objects or contamination.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And the little thermal shield that goes around the periphery of the lens barrel, we want to inspect that for contamination, and also, the lens barrel itself.
And we're really looking for contamination really on all of the Pan Camera, and the same goes for the V over H sensor.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And just to refresh your memory, the V over H sensor is the one that's on the right side that's got the little hood on it.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And for the Gamma Ray boom, what we'd like to do is inspect the cover for any damage.
You know, that thing has closed a couple of times and - or the boom has retracted all the way a couple of times and a couple of times it hasn't.
So - so we're expecting that the Gamma Ray boom right now is about 6 inches out; but we're not sure.
We'd like for you to look at the cover, and if it's not fully closed, give us an estimate of how far it's open.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: If the cover is partially open ...
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Is Brad [?] taking bets on where it is?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: He's not - he's not too brave.
He'll bet you a cup of coffee it's about 6 inches.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And we'd like to know how far that rail protrudes through the bind there, and if you can see any obstructions to retractions, you know, like the harness, or the guides, or the loom cable fingers, proximity switches, any of those things - report them.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And that's all we want to know, Ken, other than any other things you might pick up from looking around out there.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Okay; do you require any kind of documentation on this?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I guess we'll have to take verbal comment.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And, Ken, we'd like to vent the battery compart - compartment to 1 volt on your meter.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: It drops like a stone; I don't know if we can do that or not.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We got 1.2 on there; you want to press and try for 1?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And, Ken, the stellar camera's out of film, if you want to use a normal shutdown procedure on the Mapping Camera.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: He kind of would like to know if that's the first time you've noticed that Bat vent or bat - battery compartment pressure above 1.5 volts?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: John says that when they did the first charges they had to take it and vent it, but the charges I did I never saw it above 1.5.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We'll probably be asking you for a check of that here in a little while, just to make sure it's not charging way on up there again.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Okay; we reported the other times that we vented it.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I'm gonna go off the loop a couple of minutes; Phil will cover for me.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: How are you doing today, Philip?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Doing very well, sir; thank you.
transcript.speaker: Shaffer

tokens: Just wanted to hear you on the loop.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Well, always nice to have a few of those.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Are you gonna get to the mapper in little bit?
transcript.speaker: Shaffer

tokens: That was Flight Director Phil Shaffer now talking to the - to the crew of Apollo 16.
We're at 239 hours, 41 minutes [214:54] Ground Elapsed Time; and Apollo 16 now 182,904 nautical miles away from the Earth.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Could you dial in your High Gain angles for the thermal attitude?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Okay; the Bat B Charge is underway, and we've been watching this battery compartment, and we're back up to 2 now.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Hank, I don't really know what's going on with our antenna.

It's - I get you the best signal strength by going to Manual and Narrow, and every time we switch to either Auto or Reacq, why, it seems to break - break some kind of beam switch lock, or something, and we've tried going to Manual and Wide and working in through Reacq and then bringing it down and things, and that doesn't help any.
Would there be any future in trying the secondary electronics, the servos?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay; INCO says we're just right on the line there.
Why don't we just leave it in Manual and Narrow.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And, Ken, I'd appreciate it if from time to time, you'd just give me a idea of where you are in the checklist.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We is at the eat period.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We'll be ready to start in on that checklist in probably about 10 minutes or so; but it just didn't seem like it was proper to go ahead and press on the rest of the day without stopping for something.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I agree with you, and I guess we'd better terminate that Battery B Charge and keep an eye on that thing.
If it gives - gets above 3, give us a call.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Okay; why don't you call us back in about 30 minutes and ask us to look at it, because that's not the kind of position where we can keep an eye on it without making a conscious effort at it.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay; we'll give you a reminder, and if it's above 3 or in that neighborhood, we're gonna want to vent it again back down to 1.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Okay; do you want to vent it now.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We want to see if it'll stabilize.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Okay; you've got the - the Bat B Charge is terminated, and we'll watch the battery's compartment pressure.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: While you're eating there, I'll tell you a little local sports news.
The Astros have won seven in a row now, and they are tied for first place in the division.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: 16, if it's convenient, could we get a check on the Bat C voltage?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And John stuck the eyeball right closer to the meter, and it's 1.9 instead of 2.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We're going to vent the battery - the bat compartment again, it's 2 and -
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: If you want to try a vent, go ahead and see if you can stop it at 1.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: What - What's wrong if we go below 1, Hank?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Well, the problem is there, Ken, if it's - at 1 volt, we're - at 1.5 volt, we're roughly equal to cabin pressure in there and, if you go much below 1 and we do have a battery that's venting, you could lose the battery, if it goes all the way to vacuum.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: In other words, if the - if the battery - a battery has vented and the - and the vent doesn't reseal - and it - and it's not resealed, if you go to vacuum, the electrolyte couldn't go - go out of it.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: It won't affect the other batteries?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: It shouldn't affect the other batteries.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Okay, it's reading 1.2 - about 1.3, I guess.
We'll leave it at that for a few minutes.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Can y'all see the Bat Charger current down there, Hank?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: That's affirmative, and it's reading zero now, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: When I started Bat B charge, I was looking at Bat Bus B, and we got a negative amp - looked like to me a negative amps, off-scale, low on Bat B, and then I went to Bat Charger, but that should - but that didn't look right to me, so I stopped.
And then I went to Bat Charger and started it and - again, and the bat charger current went up to about 2, which - or so, which looked nominal.
The only question was that it looked like a reverse current or something, and - I guess that is true, isn't it, though, when you reverse current into a battery to charge it?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: What we saw down here, I guess, when you started the - started the charge, it - it looked like that perhaps you hadn't pulled the Bat Relay circuit breaker; then you stopped and then - then it looked normal to us.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: It's - it was open and the breaker is pulled.
Or it's closed now, since the charge is terminated.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: And they also saw some funnies in your data.
It looked like the charge started out on Bat A and then it went to Bat B.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Well, you got to go to the A position to get to B.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We're right - unstowing from A-2 into the top of page 3-2 now.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: 16, Houston, we got a - an answer to your question about the wrist rings on your suit.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We're not ready for that answer right now, thank you.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Hank, on the battery compartment, we vented back down to 1, and it's back up to about 1 - 1.3 we vented to, and it's back up to about 1.8 now and seems to be stabilized.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: We got 5900 pounds in the OPS, and it's regulating at 3.7.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: We're - Hank, we're down to the TV and DAC Prep, and we're just before installing the meter covers.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: This is Apollo Control, Houston; at 240 hours, 38 minutes [215:51] Ground Elapsed Time.
The crew of Apollo 16 presently going through preparations in the cabin for the Command Module Pilot EVA.

Our countdown clock in Mission Control going for hatch open time; we show 2 hours and 16 minutes away now and Apollo 16 is presently at a distance of 180,774 nautical miles away from the Earth, velocity now reading 3,850 feet per second.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Ken, we'd like to see the turnoff of the SIM bay that's on 3-6 before you turn our data off.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Understand; the SIM bay turnoff on 3-6 before we turn data off.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And we'll need that data switch - the S-Band Aux TV switch in SCI in order to see the data.
And we have some additions to that turnoff, too.
Whenever you're ready to do some of those, I'll call them out to you.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We want the Mapping Camera, Off; the Gamma Ray, Off; and we want to verify that the Mass Spec, even though we don't have it, we want to verify that the Mass Spec experiment is Off, and the Ion Source is Off; Laser Altimeter, Off; and Image Motion, Off.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And all of those items are in addition to the ones that are listed on page 3-6.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Okay, that's where they'll go, on page 3-6.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: This is Apollo Control, Houston, at 240 hours, 46 minutes [215:59], Ground Elapsed Time, very little discussion with the crew of Apollo 16.
Since the crew is presently going through preparations for the Command Module Pilot's trip outside the spacecraft.
We show Apollo 16 at a distance away from [Earth] of 180,489 nautical miles, velocity now reads 3,854 feet per second.
Continuing to monitor; this is Apollo Control, Houston.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Could you give us a check on the battery compartment now?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Okay, it's up to 2.0, just a little higher than it was last time.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: This is Apollo Control, Houston; at 240 hours, 49 minutes [216:02] Ground Elapsed Time, The discussion we've had with regard to the batteries, we're talking about entry batteries, and exploring the possibility of one of the 3 - we do monitor batteries A and B, but not battery C via telemetry, however, from our discussion here, is centered around data not covered via telemetry.
Each battery has 20 cells, much like a car battery but with check valves rather than caps for each cell.
Right now we don't believe that that we have a problem with any one of these cells, but we're exploring that kind of possibility with the pressure built up.
It could indicate the possibility of one of the cells - one of the batteries, could be venting.
I repeat, we have no reason to believe that right now, but we're exploring that with the crew of Apollo 16.

The principle concern does not deal with battery capability, but rather to make sure that battery gas would not be relieving into the cabin, Entry can be safely accomplished on only one of the 3 batteries.
We're at 240 hours, 51 minutes [216:04], we now show Apollo 16 at a distance of 180,309 nautical miles away from the Earth and travelling at a speed of 3,857 feet per second.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: We're on the bottom of page 3-4, and we're taking the couch out now.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: This is Apollo Control, Houston, 241 hours Ground Elapsed Time [216:13].
That report indicated that the crew of Apollo 16 is now removing the center couch in preparation of - for the EVA.
We presently show Apollo 16 at a distance from Earth of 179,956 nautical miles, velocity now reads 3,862 feet per second.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: When you get around to - prior to suit donning, we recommend that you lubricate those wrist rings using the maintenance kit that's stored in A-8.
And you might try to work some of that lube in around the locking rings and work the ring several times to try to free them up a little bit - the locking rings.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: we did that when we doffed the suits yesterday as best we could.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'm not sure it had much effect, but we did it.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Charlie, did you do the full lubing and cleaning, including the zippers, in accordance with the instructions in the maintenance kit?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Just like we done it on the surface.
We did the zippers, all of the O-rings, the neck rings, the wrist rings, and it seems to me, Hank, it's really not where the O-ring is.
It's in the sliding part between the suit part and the locking part of it.
You could see dust down in there, but you can't get it out too well.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Only thing we could recommend there, which I'm sure you've already done, is just sock it a few times - see if you can free it up.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We'd like to get another read-out on the battery compartment, and we suggest you leave the meter setting there so we can read it during the EVA if we have to - or you can read it.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: At 241 hours, 21 minutes [216:34] Ground Elapsed Time.
The Science Staff Support room has just reported that the sub-satellite has been activated and it's working fine.
The sub-satellite in orbit around the Moon.
We now show Apollo 16 at 179,143 nautical miles away from the Earth and travelling at a speed of 38,075 [sic, should be 3,875] feet per second.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: For your information, we just commanded the Sub-sat on, and it's alive and well.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Okay Houston, we're just about to start with the - with the SIM bay turnoff here on Page 3-6, and we'll do those additions that you talked about.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And, 16, before you start your suit donning, we'd like to vent that battery compartment one more time down to about 1 volt.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We're on page 3-6, and we're starting our PGA donning right now.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I think we've made up 45 minutes on our late wakeup - hour late wakeup this morning.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And we're standing by for that battery compartment reading.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Charlie just superbly vented it to 1.0.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Ken's completed donning his suit now, and Charlie's going to start on his.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: This is Apollo Control, Houston; at 141 hours [means 241 hours], 49 minutes [217:02] Ground Elapsed Time.
That was Ken Mattingly calling Hank Hartsfield, our CapCom here in Mission Control.
Mattingly [is] now scheduled to move outside the spacecraft in one hour and five minutes.
Earlier John Young had reported that Ken Mattingly had completed the donning of his space suit and that Lunar Module Pilot Charles Duke was in the process of donning his space suit.
We show Apollo 16 at a distance of 178,085 nautical miles away from the Earth and travelling at a speed of 3,891 feet per second.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Hank, instead of making this maneuver to the EVA attitude at 242:00, suppose we do it as soon as we finish suit donning.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: You can do that whenever you're ready.
When you do do the maneuver, we'd like for you to go Wide on the High Gain.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And, Ken, in regard to the - the attitude and angle changes, - the only thing on your EVA cue card that - that I saw that required changing was the High-Gain angles.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Say again what you had to say about the attitudes now, please.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I read up the new attitudes and High-Gain angles to Charlie, and he was going to change the checklist and the cue card; and I just wanted to say that the only place on the cue cards I see that needs a change is the back side of your EVA card, the High-Gain angles right by the 50 18.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: I've got that, and - it was minus 24 and 220; and now we want something different, huh?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Now I've got a minus 34 and 218.
Shouldn't that track throughout the maneuver?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We hope it'll work in Auto TRACK, Ken, but this is just a precaution.
We've been having a little trouble with that High Gain.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: And, Hank, on that - on that battery thing, it's back up to about 1.6 now.
It came right up to 1.5, which it - that you're - that means that it's up to 1.6.
Do you want us to vent that thing during the EVA to keep from getting excessive pressure in that chamber, while we have the cabin depressed?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We'd like to watch it awhile.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: That battery compartment will take it when we get the cabin down, right?
Or at least relieve through the cabin?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: That manifold should take 200 psi, Ken.
That was what it was qual'ed at, and the burst pressure is 600.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: You guys think there's a relief valve on one of those batteries that's [garble]?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We kind of think that may be what's happening.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Do you have any evidence yet as to which battery?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: All those batteries are manifolded together, and all the voltages look normal.
If we do drop a cell on one of the batteries, we should be able to pick that up.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: This is Apollo Control, Houston at 242 hours [217:13] Ground Elapsed Time, that was Ken Mattingly reporting that Lunar Module pilot Charles Duke has suited.
We're 54 minutes away now from time of hatch open.
We show Apollo 16 at an altitude of 177,691 nautical miles and with a velocity of 3,898 feet per second.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Go Wide on the High Gain.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Hank, I just hit the yaw knob.
What are the settings in our present attitude?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: 16, we'd like to stay Wide and Manual in the High Gain.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Are you going to want that during the EVA, Hank?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: That's affirmative, and we'll call it when we start the maneuver.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Wide gives you enough margin on the TV?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: That was really a question, Hank.
(Laughing) I didn't mean it as a statement.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: What's going to happen is: when we do get in attitude, we can bring it back to Auto and get it locked up; but we will need the Narrow Beam.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Because that's a - that's going to be a very difficult thing to try and tweak up in a hard suit.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We're noticing that the glycol evap out temp is coming on down, and we need to adjust that mixing valve in.

But rather than crawling under there and doing that right now, what we want to do is get you to take the Temp In switch from Manual up to Auto, and we'll give you a call when to put it back in Manual and see if that will cut it.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Is that going to have enough time delay - on your call that's going to - I guess - I guess I'm concerned about making it worse.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We're going to have to adjust it one way or the other, Ken, and we're going to lead it.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We can always go back and do it manually if it doesn't work.
Do you want to do that - let's do it right now before our maneuver and make sure we don't lose comm in the middle of it.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We'd like to do it now ...
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Ken, I know this is going to sound funny, but it looks like that mixing valve is working now.
It didn't go up like we thought it would.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: But we're going to have to make a configuration setting before we start the EVA, because we won't be able to get to it then.
If we can find a place where it looks good, I'd like to leave it in Manual throughout the EVA, and we can try the Auto feature some other time.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We'd like to put the switch back to Manual and maneuver to the EVA attitude, and we'll take a look when we get good comm there.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: The Temp In switch is in Manual, and I'm going to the EVA attitude.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Hank, we'll be switching comm here again for a minute.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Apollo 16, if you read, Omni Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: We're down to Page 3-8 on the pressure gage static check we're running through now, Hank.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Could you acquire with your High Gain?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Would you go Reacq and Narrow?
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: This is Apollo Control Houston at 242 hours, 29 minutes [217:42] Ground Elapsed Time.
The Apollo 16 spacecraft has now been maneuvered to a proper EVA attitude and we have acquired with the High Gain Antenna.

We show 25 minutes remaining on our countdown clock to time of hatch open, and we show Apollo 16 at a distance of 176,589 nautical miles away from the Earth and travelling at a speed of 39015 [sic, should be 3,915] feet per second.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: And for your information, the mixing valve setting is good.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Are you going to check - going to check out the TV?
Let me see if I got a picture here.
You got a - got anything on there yet?
You got a - the monitor's working, isn't it?
That is the end of it.
Get your monitor adjusted so it shows you what you'd like.
I'll open it up so you can see something inside, and I'll close it down before we go outside.
Take the zoom all the way out.
Set the zoom back to where it was supposed to be.
Houston, are you getting a picture?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: We won't worry about dressing it up till we get outside.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Let me put this back up.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Command Module Pilot Ken Mattingly now checking out the TV system in preparation for the EVA.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: We got the TV power off, did you say?
And the camera's back in place.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: You read me okay on the Cdr's loop?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Houston's reading you five by five, John.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: It's going back Off, and the tone is off.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: You can call Verb 49, and it will already be loaded.
You've got to Pro out of this.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: It may be out of attitude, but - Okay [garble].
take two Pro's to go to the MEED Attitude.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: No, that acts as a supplement to the Surge tank.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: This is Apollo Control Houston at 242 hours, 42 minutes [217:55] Ground Elapsed Time.
Apollo 16 now at a distance of 176,093 nautical miles away from the Earth, and travelling at a speed of 3,924 feet per second.
The integrity checks for the suit still needs to be completed.
We will standby until we have a more definite time for hatch open and pass that along a soon as it comes available.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Can you get this in your ...
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: You want me to stuff it in my TSB?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: I'll tell you what, I'm gonna put it up here just - I don't want to lose this one.
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, that's Flight Plan is stowed in R3.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And I have the valve in the open position, and would you verify that that's open.
Counter clockwise - No, what does it say on the arrow?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Apollo Control Houston at 242 hours, 45 minutes [217:58] Ground Elapsed Time.
Our best estimate for hatch open at this point approximately 40 minutes and the countdown clock in Mission Control being reflected to show that hatch open time.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: My hoses are disconnected and stowed.
Suit flow is Off, the interconnect is in.
It's locked on two sides, and stowed on the strut.

tokens: Okay, I've got the adapter plate on.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: And I've unconnected the - disconnected the OPS hose.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: This is Apollo Control Houston, at 242 hours, 49 minutes [218:02]Ground Elapsed Time, Ken Mattingly has just connected the OPS hose with the assistance of Lunar Module Pilot Charles Duke.
We show Apollo 16 at a distance of 175,810 nautical miles from Earth.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: I want to go over the umbilical.
The OPS is installed, the gas connector is installed, and it's locked.
May have to disconnect this thing to get it down there.
Oh, I'm sorry, I didn't hear you.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: But - Will you watch cabin pressure for me?
Want me to read those while you guys do the integrity check?
You want me to read those things while you - you have both hands -
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Hey, where's - where's the other - Here's your helmet, where's my - laid it down.
I'll get it out for y'all.
Those are your gloves, Charlie, or - okay.
Okay, my gloves should be down there to your right somewhere, I think.
Yeah, [garble] get them out of the way.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: This is Apollo Control Houston at 242 hours and 53 minutes [218:06] Ground Elapsed Time.
We're about 30 minutes away now from hatch open.
It's taking a little bit longer than had originally been anticipated to go through the checklist procedures in getting ready for the Command Module Pilot's EVA.
We're at 242 hours, 54 minutes Ground Elapsed Time.
We show Apollo 16 at a distance of 175,657 nautical miles.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: Get this little strap on here, and we'll be all set.
I will not steal the pen.
That looks clear in the back.
Put your head over here a second.
Yeah, that's - The little skirt around the back gets in the way [garble].
Want to take the LEWA off so we can get more pressure on it?
Goes on so much easier than it comes off.
That about in the center for you?

tokens: Charlie's donning his helmet and gloves with the - for the suit integrity check we're about to get into here for CDR/LMP.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That one's locked, and your helmet is locked.
I'll hold it out of the way for you.

tokens: Both of you checked the connections, comm, oxygen and gas connector plugs.

I don't think you do on the cue card.

transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: The Suit Pressure indicator is reading 5.2 - it's about 5.

I have the Equalisation Valve, Open a little bit.
Will you keep an eye on the cabin pressure for us, please?

tokens: Would you vent it down to about 5.17 It's up now.

tokens: Would you keep an eye on the cabin pressure, please?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: The flow is normal; suit pressure, okay.
We're ready to go on to suit circuit integrity check.
And Suit Test valve to Press.

transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I want to get it up to -
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Okay, why don't you cycle the Suit Circuit Return valve ?

tokens: Your suit pressure is still only about 1.5; have to turn that thing up some to get it a little ...
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, I'll get it up there.

tokens: And, Hank, it looks like we're gonna be about 10 minutes late on hatch open.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Call it off to me, Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: When do you want me to shut the Direct O2off, Ken?
transcript.speaker: Duke

tokens: Okay, why don't you turn the Direct O2off?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, it - it ought to get up to almost 4.5.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Yeah, that's where it regulates to, right there, about 4.5.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: And the flow should start to drop here.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, Houston, how long does it take before the flow starts to drop on this integrity check?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Get you a little Delta-P there, probably help.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: That's all it needed was that bump, that little bit.
Okay, I'm turning my flow off -
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, and my check is that the flow remains stable and low.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I wouldn't consider it low, Ken; it's climbing right back up.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: In other words, it doesn't peg.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, the flow is holding right now at 0.72 and - on me and Charlie.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Now it's pegged; for some reason, it just pegged.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Yeah, that's probably what it was, 8, 9, 10 - Yep, there's a cyclic accumulator; hmm.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: Roger; we confirm that down here.
transcript.speaker: Hartsfield

tokens: Why don't you just go to Depress on the Suit Test valve?
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Okay, you'll come down pretty slow [garble].
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I won't come down at all, unless I can find the valve.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I tell you, it's pretty good for these old suits to be holding air with all this Moon dust in them, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That was John Young making that call out.
We're at 243 hours, 7 minutes [218:20] Ground Elapsed Time.
We show Apollo 16 at an altitude of 175,129 nautical miles.
transcript.speaker: PAO

tokens: I got my Purge valve pin pulled.
And my Purge valve is activated to LOW.
The Diverter valves are rechecked into vertical.
We get your suits back down and get the systems stable, we'll go to the EVA card.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Well, it's comforting to know that there's some sunlight out there.
When we - when we - vented the cabin, some of the particles went out, and you could see the sunlight out on them.
Otherwise, it's just black as all get out.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Hey, you want to open that black bag down there and put a vent on it?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: I - I got bare hands here.
Can you - Snap's all the way in the back.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Are we in this card here?
transcript.speaker: Young

tokens: That, - that card picks up as soon as I get this thing back to - get you back to normal on this ...
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Now I'm turning my O2flow back on.
Houston, can keep an eye on the cabin pressure for us, if you will, please.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: I just turned my flow back on, so it'll start to rise again.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: Got about three-quarters of a psi to go.
transcript.speaker: Mattingly

tokens: This is Apollo Control, Houston, at 243 hours, 24 minutes [218:25]Ground Elapsed Time.
Ken Mattingly now in the process of...

tokens: [Garble] and get us little head start so we don't get interrupted on our check.

tokens: ...donning his helmet and gloves at this time.
We show Apollo 16 at a distance of 174,983 nautical miles.

tokens: Okay, your equalization valve is closed.

tokens: I'm going to Suit Test valve to Off.

tokens: Cabin Regs are in Both - I mean, the Demand Regs.

Want it turned around the other way?

tokens: Well, we're going to need it during the - I'm going to put it right in here.
Okay, John, I'm ready to press on.

tokens: And these stripes ought to line up pretty good, do they?

tokens: Hey, your lock stripe is to the - about - you got to come this way - the other way.

tokens: Okay, don wrist tether, ring forward.

tokens: I've got it on my glove.

tokens: Don LEVA - that's done - Verify alignment.

tokens: All right, panel EVA O2, On.
Let me verify those locks again.

tokens: Here comes the right - Well, I'll turn it Off.

tokens: Okay, the flow is coming back On.

tokens: Modulate On and Off as required to pressurize the CMP.

tokens: Don't I do a suit integrity check, John?

tokens: I'm sorry, I didn't turn the flow Off.
Okay, my flow is coming Off.
Nope, it's going up the [garble] there.
